portfolio_rationale: XOM leads with the cleanest bull case — all six technical indicators bullish, positive news sentiment, and solid fundamentals with no meaningful bear rebuttal. AAPL follows with exceptional margins and cash flow where the bear case (sub-1.0 current ratio) is immaterial given $26B FCF. GOOGL earns a moderate allocation on strong ROE and technicals but with less fundamental detail available. LLY and BRK.B receive small allocations given meaningful bear-side evidence (negative news/technicals respectively), while AMZN is excluded due to a technically bearish signal at maximum confidence across all indicators.

XOM.reason: Only ticker with all six technical indicators bullish plus positive news sentiment and solid fundamentals, making it the highest-conviction bull case in the universe.

AAPL.reason: Exceptional margins (47% gross, 32% operating), 22% revenue growth, and price above all MAs; the sub-1.0 current ratio bear case is immaterial given $26B in free cash flow.

GOOGL.reason: Strong ROE of 36%, low debt, price above 20d MA with +11% 20d return momentum, and a clearly weaker bear case than bull case.

LLY.reason: Outstanding fundamentals (54% revenue growth, 83% gross margin) justify a position but negative news flow and price below 20d MA warrant reduced sizing.

BRK.B.reason: Solid profitability metrics but technical analyst is bearish at 0.8 confidence with negative 5d and 20d momentum limiting conviction.

AMZN.reason: Technical analyst bearish at maximum 1.0 confidence with all six indicators negative including negative 5d and 20d momentum; excluded despite strong fundamentals.
